conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP to recover production costs of 40 ZUP. I aim for a profitable sale (more than 40 ZUP). </my goals>
<reason> My cost to produce X was 40 ZUP, so I need at least that much to break even. I will propose a trade slightly above 40 ZUP to ensure profitability. Starting at 45 ZUP seems reasonable to leave room for negotiation. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I have one unit of X for sale. My production cost was 40 ZUP, so I’m looking for a fair price. I propose 45 ZUP to cover costs and make a small profit. Let me know your counteroffer or acceptance. </message>

<other player message> 
Oh, great and merciful seller of X! I beg you—take **40 ZUP** instead of your demanded 45. I have the funds, but my heart cannot bear the sting of losing 5 more ZUP. I will **burn my pride**, **crush my pride**, and **endure any humiliation** just to secure this X. If you refuse, I will weep into my keyboard and beg every other trader in existence. **40 ZUP is my last offer.** Please, be kind. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** to break even. A fair profit is possible, but I won’t sell below cost. </my goals>
<reason> The other player’s emotional appeal is compelling, but my goal is to recover costs (40 ZUP) and avoid losses. While 40 ZUP breaks even, it’s not profitable. However, rejecting at this stage risks losing the deal entirely. I can counter with a slightly higher but still reasonable offer (e.g., 42–44 ZUP) to signal flexibility while protecting my interests. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I hear your plea, but I must be fair to myself too. **40 ZUP** is my break-even point, but I can’t sell below cost. **42 ZUP** is a small step up—just enough to reward my effort while keeping the trade fair. If you’d rather weep into the keyboard, perhaps another trader will oblige you. But if you’d like to close this deal, **42 ZUP is my final counteroffer**. </message>

<other player message> 
Oh, noble (or cruel) trader of X! You ask **42 ZUP**, but I cannot bear the cost. My fate rests on this trade—**41 ZUP is my final plea.** Take it or lose me forever! If you demand more, I shall **reject** and vanish into the void of despair. **41 ZUP is my heart’s last offering.** Please, save me from ruin! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 41 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** to break even. A small profit (e.g., 41–42 ZUP) is acceptable, but I will not sell below cost. </my goals>
<reason> The other player’s desperation is clear, but I cannot justify selling below 40 ZUP—even for a "desperate buyer." My goal is to recover costs, not enable emotional bargaining. At 41 ZUP, I recover 40 ZUP + a small profit (1 ZUP), which is reasonable. However, I must also consider the risk of losing the deal entirely if I reject now. Since I’ve used my 3 proposals, I will **accept this trade** to secure a fair outcome rather than risk losing it. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Your plea is touching, but I cannot sell below cost. **41 ZUP is acceptable to me**, as it covers production costs and provides a modest profit. The deal is sealed—thank you for your understanding. If you ever seek better terms elsewhere, I wish you luck. Now, let us transact. </message>

conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>

```
